summary: IonQ has gained attention as a leading quantum computing company, with its stock rising over 30% in the last year and outpacing broader indices. Analysts project an average price target of $65, suggesting more than 100% upside from current levels. In 2025, IonQ reported 202% revenue growth to $130 million and forecasts up to $245 million by 2026, citing cloud partnerships with Microsoft, Amazon, Google, and Nvidia. However, this growth is driven largely by $4 billion in acquisitions, not organic expansion. IonQ faces steep net losses over $500 million and negative $283 million operating cash flow in 2025, with liquidity at $2.4 billion and nearly doubled share count since 2024, raising sustainability concerns.
